reviews.review_title: Really love the game con listed below
reviews.review_description: It was really buggy but with the world but not that bad and disliked the way the beginning was done everything else was pretty good I hope there sequel of this game please take note to make it more open and fun

Very slight spoiler warning for the ending (after final level) 
I honestly like the idea of this idea, bow is the only weapon, everybody's gone, and random voice guides you towards through robots

The game has slight Metroid vibe where you find something, have a new level but in the same area and you can proceed there

The game is enjoyable but the inventory is a little...unusual, you get easily stuck into a staircase, sometimes and Im pretty sure teleporting with an item in your hand makes it disappear or just fly somewhere you won't find (happened to a clipboard with a code)

Now the game would have 5 stars if not for this next flaw... The game doesn't end, after you finish the final level and level ending happen, you just go back to the safe house, no credits, no new levels, no congrats for finishing the game amd Fathr just says the same thing when going to level selection... The game feels incomplete so if you actually care about the ending, you WILL get disapointed and you should think twice about getting the game

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.
Game glitches way to much
This game would be fun is it was co-op so you can play with friends so if that is anyway to update it to make is Co-op that would be nice

reviews.review_title: Few glitches, but that doesn’t break it
reviews.review_description:
I like the story mode, and the challenges.  But there are some glitches, when you come to close to a wall.
I had hoped to finish the game, but I got stuck on the 3th core. That battle is hard. Was playing on easy mode.
Graphics are great. I enjoyed playing it.
i had this game on my watchlist from the oculus quest days, i finally brought it, but its way too buggy and simply doesn't hold up in today's market. Also the ending is super anti climactic!

Come on look at it 
best game i got for its price

reviews.review_title: Not a bad game when it works
reviews.review_description: I quite enjoyed it at start, but found way too many bugs. I don't mind revisiting the same place multiple times (like others do) as long as the story goes along with it, which is the case here. There are many many spots where you can leave the playable area (which gives room for some noclip discoveries) - if you do like some exploration like this, then prepare to abandon the level as sometimes you won't be able to find your way back. What the most annoying to me was that putting down the headset (Quest 3) a few times to take some breaks will eventually lead to software hang, which cannot be solved by quitting and restarting. You need to reboot the headset as the task remains in the background as a zombie. Is there a task manager for Q3? Losing gameplay like this annoys me like not many things. Also there are bugs like unable to walk stairs, getting stuck in walls / opened doors/cabinets. The inventory is working on a trial-and-error basis. Many times i tried to grab something in it, but instead the game teleported the board of note in my hand from the floor. One time i gave up on finding a code and checking it on a walkthrough video just to find out that the cabinet holding the note with the code is not there in my game. It was there upon some next visit later on. I can enjoy this game, when it works, but i cannot give it 5 stars while these bugs are there.
I like it. It’s like In Death with a story and item management. This game has pretty good archery combat and a cool Walking Dead like inventory.
There is no "Wow!" with this game. Skip it unless you like dental work as well.

This game might have done enough to impress on release in 2019, but underwhelms now.  Sure it has a full narrative campaign, combat, physics interactions, etc. but it doesn't execute on any of them particularly well.  Levels are extremely limited in size and most of the gameplay involves traipsing room to room hunting notepads of 4 digit codes so you can unlock a locked door to progress, with some mediocre bow combat thrown in between. And despite the small level size, you will revisit each location 2-3 times during the story, forcing you to investigate the exact same rooms for new codes each time.  The game does feature BHaptics vest support, which is cool and the reason I picked it up, but even this underwhelms as the effects are used so sparingly my vest timed out for inactivity.  Unfortunately, not every older game can be a timeless classic that warrants revisiting. There are simply more exciting options out there nowadays.

I really enjoyed this game, great game mechanics even for an older vr game, which honestly doesn’t matter. 

The story is also fun, and I liked its pacing. 

Great work!

How can you release a game that doesn't have a proper ending? 1 star until you complete the game.

UPDATE: Judging from the generic answers from the developers about this matter, it seems like they have no actual interest in completing the game.

reviews.review_title: looks nice but not very fun
reviews.review_description: The environment is big but only sparsely interesting. It's full of grabbable objects that doesn't do anything. The "father" figure sounds like he needs to clear his throat. Technically the graphics look good (for its time), but it seems to get boring fast.
Very good game, I recommend it to everyone, thank you very much.

Got this game because it was in sale for 1$. It sounded interesting, and the video play looked fun, and a dollar was pocket change. It's definitely a very fun game and story. Reminds me of the portal games that I love so much.

reviews.review_title: Needs codes that can't he found
reviews.review_description:
This game is ok, but it's really unintuitive and demands codes and answers to puzzles that don't even exist. In the level called "Another Toy", there is a code required to open a door to get an overcharger. The code is nowhere to be found in the rest of the level. I had to reference a walkthrough to get the code and proceed, which I hate doing.

Totally unacceptable in a puzzle game.
It's good up to a point. You try to fallow the story but doesn't tell you where to go. And the puzzles are also really hard to under stand. But that might just be me. Either way I didn't like it.

I am writing this at the launch of the 3s. I am playing on the Quest 2. Pros: There are clear mission breaks and each mission takes 10-30 minutes depending on skill and knowledge. This makes it great to pick up and play a short section if you just need a short break. The bow and arrow is responsive and feels like a skill weapon. When I miss an enemy I don't feel like the game is at fault.
Cons: There is little variety. Most enemies are the same bot, but the bot is stronger or has a shield, or can teleport. You have 3 arrow types but will usually use the default arrow. There are grenades, but you will rarely use them.
This is a good 'tech demo' game. It feels good to play for the beginning game and has enough story to hold it together. The addition of an archery range makes it easy to show off to a friend. For the price it is a decent addition to your game library.

I enjoyed playing this one although it has some anoying bugs here and there (played the PC version since this is a cross-buy and looked a little better) and the end is very disappointing (leaves all the questions open, so all the story elements are basically for nothing).
Stupid

I did enjoy the game, and I agree with many of the reviews. I did not finish it though because I was missing access card that I was supposed to get during previous level, but that didn't happen, and I'm just not playing the previous level again, as I didn't like that particular one very much.

That said, I got this game on sale for $7, and for that price it's GREAT, even if I didn't finish it.

The bow mechanics work well, and there is a story, though I felt there was a long gap before learning something new.

The one less star is for the technical state, which I was hoping would be more ironed out since the game has been out for some time now.

Cheers!

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts & feedback! If you decide you would like to complete it, send us an email over at support@fasttravelgames.com & we can point you in the direction of where the access card is :-)

reviews.review_description:
I would give this game 4.5 stars if I could.I know I should probably write this review after I’m more than two levels in(which I am).However I have some things to say.1 the voice acting is great and the story is intriguing and mysterious,and I’ll likely still be doing some theorizing even after I’ve finished the game.The combat is excellent,the enimies’ designs are fantastic,and the the world is captivating,the world design captivating and beautiful,and I just think the terminals scattered throughout said world are neat.But the one thing I like most….Is how almost every and I mean ALMOST EVER single button and switch can be interacted with,even if no visual response is shown on most at least there is a little clicking sound,and it would be interesting if there were achievements on oculus and one that one for this game was interacting with,if not ever then almost, every single 
button.However as with all games there are a few flaws I noticed from the get-go.For one(and really my main problem) is direction isn’t always clear.You can’t easily tell where to go or what terminals you need to interact with or what areas you can’t yet reach until later(as a final side note on the second level near the player’s first encounter with mother you have to get two batteries,and some one could get stuck for a very long time looking for the button that opens the bathroom.)But those are all my complaints and for the where to go issue that can be fixed by either adding an  arrow that points to the next objective or by adding more of the little gold circle thingies

developer_response.body: Wow thank you Jbuddy321 for an awesome in depth review! We're happy to hear you're having a great time playing so far!

reviews.review_title: This is what I expected VR to be.
reviews.review_description: I'm new to VR with this being one of my first games. It's an awesome experience. This one will be a favorite for sure.

developer_response.body:
Welcome to the world of VR! Thanks so much for this lovely review, we're so happy to hear how much you enjoyed playing!

Feel free to check out our other titles The Curious Tale of the Stolen Pets (puzzle adventure game) or Wraith: The Oblivion - Afterlife (horror exploration game)!

reviews.review_description: I'm new to vr and this game is pretty cool...its a beautiful but hostile world...good luck

developer_response.body: We really appreciate the review! Thanks for taking the time to submit it :-)

reviews.review_title: I wish I could leave 0 stars
reviews.review_description: This game is the definition of steaming hot garbage

reviews.review_title: No joke don’t waste ur time/money
reviews.review_description: This is the first game I’ve hated in vr there are so many reasons why this game is bad but I’ve already wasted enough energy with this game to write review on how bad it is. only reason I played it though cause oculus wouldn’t refund me don’t waste ur money wouldn’t recommend this game even if it was free to play

developer_response.body:
Hi,
We're sorry to hear that you don't enjoy the game. It would be really appreciated if you could give some examples of the things you didn't like. We really want to know!

reviews.review_title: Decent Bow mechanics, story is ok so far
reviews.review_description: Game feels a little clunky with everything other than bow mechanics. Enemies feel pretty repetitive quickly. There are better stories on VR and better combat mechanics on VR to be found elsewhere. Worth it to wait for a sale here, it leaves more to be desired.
So far I can not see why this was a good buy. Maybe one day soon I could write a better review.

Picked this up as my first full VR game. Played through the game on hard and it was definitely difficult AMD entertaining. Very immersive and the bow and arrow combat is pretty satisfying once you get the hang of it. The environments are pretty awesome to explore and the game is well optimized.

developer_response.body: We are so happy you enjoyed it, and as your first full VR game as well! Amazing to hear!

reviews.review_description: I first played this game on Nov 18 2020 and it’s definitely worth every penny. Besides the fun of being able to explore, shoot robots with a bow, and unravel an intriguing storyline, one of the best features is the in-game locomotion. Besides the now common teleportation method of getting around, Apesx construct allows you to move freely using room scale, and provides a stepped “turn view” feature which allows you to recenter your view with the flick of a joystick. So you can walk to the edge of the guardian, turn 180°, flip a switch and just keep walking. It’s the first game I’ve tried that allows you to literally walk through almost the entire game, which makes for an incredibly immersive experience.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the wonderful review and for taking the time to share your feedback with us!

reviews.review_description: Can't believe the story just ends like that. There are still way too many mysteries unsolved. The gameplay itself is quite ok, but the story is just way too short. I didn't even enjoy it enough. The conflict between the Mothr and Fathr remains unknown; why the Shifts came into existence remains unknown; who else were sacrifice before the character we play appears remain unknown. Just wish there are more stages, bigger maps, bigger universes for us to explore

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to leave a review, we appreciate getting insight into what our players think.

reviews.review_description: I saw this game and immediately fell in love. amazing story, combat, and sound design. Failing doesn't punish you beyond reason, but still gives a reason to try. Definitely buy this!

developer_response.body: So happy to hear you liked it!

reviews.review_description: Creepy and tense but not too overwhelming. Clever puzzles, intriguing world. The archery feels really good.

developer_response.body: We're so happy to hear you enjoyed the game! Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts with us :-)

reviews.review_description: I have got a lot of good game play out of this game. There are a couple spots that have bugs and I had to restart the game. Overall I am impressed with it and it keeps me entertained.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for taking the time to leave a review! We're happy to hear you enjoyed playing!

reviews.review_description: The game is fun but it’s quite buggy, quite a few times I’ll get stuck because the beam at the end of a level wouldn’t show up.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! Please can you email us at support@fasttravelgames.com with further information and we will investigate into why this is happening :-)

reviews.review_description:
It's a good enough looking game with an interesting broken world aesthetic.  But I found the hit boxes of the enemies frustrating, and the bow and arrow gameplay tedious.  It's not uncommon at all to swear you hit an enemy, only to see the arrow appear to pass right through an appendage or coif.

The story is once again, ok.  It doesn't really come to any real or satisfying conclusion or reveal.

It's also a bit of a let down that you'll mostly revisit the same handful of maps across 12 missions.  Altered by your previous missions there, granted.  But still, the exploration was my favorite part of this game, and revisiting the same maps so often really put a crimp in it.

If this weren't a launch game, it's be utterly unremarkable.  But since it's here, it's ok I guess.

The game is great! It’s also great to acclimate people to VR. Because it has shooting, puzzle solving, and exploring. Sometimes I can even get a little creeped out! It’s a great game highly recommended.

developer_response.body: We really appreciate the review, thanks for taking the time to leave it! We are glad to hear you enjoyed playing!

reviews.review_description:
Apex Construct is a pretty bland game in all regards. Featuring a bare bones, poorly written, narrative and incredibly basic gameplay. The only thing this game really has going for it is the fact that it's a VR game.

If you're new to VR and this game is on sale, maybe consider picking it up. It could serve as an effective first experience to get comfortable with VR gameplay. Though I would recommend titles like Beat Saber and Superhot VR over Apex Construct to those in that position.

However, if you've had a VR system for a while and are fairly comfortable with it I'd avoid this game. There are much better titles available for Oculus that do what Apex Construct tries to do to a much higher degree of success.

developer_response.body: We are sad to hear you found Apex Construct to be bland, but we appreciate you taking the time to submit your feedback! It has not gone unnoticed.
